fab_section_md:
- Queen holding King's Landing, growing ever more isolated by betrayal (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:17)
- By arresting Addam Velaryon she lost a dragon, a dragonrider, and her Queen's Hand, and more than half her army abandoned her (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:21)
- She imprisoned Corlys Velaryon under the Red Keep (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:21)
- She named Ser Glendon Goode Lord Commander of the Queensguard and gave command of the gold cloaks to Ser Balon Byrch (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:97)
- She forbade her son Joffrey to leave the castle, then lost Syrax and Joffrey together (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:103)
- Beset on every side, she fled King's Landing through the Dragon Gate toward Duskendale (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:245)
identity_line: Rhaenyra Targaryen — Queen holding King's Landing, growing ever more isolated by betrayal.

fab_section_md:
- Sister, wife, and queen to King Aegon II and mother of his children, aged one-and-twenty (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:23)
- She threw herself from a window in Maegor's Holdfast onto the iron spikes below (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:23)
- According to Mushroom she was with child; Munkun blames the horror of the knights' deaths; Septon Eustace says Mysaria told her of her son Maelor's death (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:25)
- At her death her dragon Dreamfyre snapped two of her chains atop the Hill of Rhaenys (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:39)
identity_line: Helaena Targaryen — Sister, wife, and queen to King Aegon II and mother of his children, aged one-and-twenty.

fab_section_md:
- Forbidden to leave, he stole onto Syrax without saddle or whip, meaning to reach his own dragon Tyraxes (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:157)
- Syrax, wanting no other rider, twisted beneath him and he plunged two hundred feet to his death in Flea Bottom (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:163)
- He is named the last of Rhaenyra's sons by Laenor Velaryon, or her bastard by Ser Harwin Strong, depending which truth one believes (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:171)
identity_line: Joffrey Velaryon — Forbidden to leave, he stole onto Syrax without saddle or whip, meaning to reach his own dragon Tyraxes.

fab_section_md:
- After Joffrey's death he was her last living son, whom she clutched fiercely (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:229)
- He fled the city with her (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:247)
identity_line: Aegon the Younger — After Joffrey's death he was her last living son, whom she clutched fiercely.

fab_section_md:
- Ser; commander of the gold cloaks, named in rumor as Helaena's killer, though he was demonstrably eating with three hundred gold cloaks at the time (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:39)
- He led the City Watch against the Shepherd's mob at Cobbler's Square (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:63)
identity_line: Luthor Largent — Ser; commander of the gold cloaks, named in rumor as Helaena's killer, though he was demonstrably eating with three hundred gold cloaks at the time.

fab_section_md:
- He directed the mob to storm the Dragonpit and slay the dragons (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:121)
- Though he held sway over tens of thousands he never claimed kingship, styling himself a simple son of the Seven (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:269)

- A hedge knight who crowned his own squire Trystane, declaring him a natural son of King Viserys (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:85)
- He knighted any sellsword and thief who flocked to Trystane's banner (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:85)
- He recruited gold cloaks and took four of the city's seven gates for Trystane (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:281)
identity_line: Perkin the Flea — A hedge knight who crowned his own squire Trystane, declaring him a natural son of King Viserys.

fab_section_md:
- The White Worm, Lady Misery, mistress of whisperers, who received the betrayal of the rescue plot (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:21)
- Taken while fleeing, the White Worm of Lys was whipped naked through the city until she died on the cobblestones (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:255)
identity_line: Mysaria — The White Worm, Lady Misery, mistress of whisperers, who received the betrayal of the rescue plot.

fab_section_md:
- According to Mushroom he originated the slander that Rhaenyra murdered Helaena (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:35)
- He emerged intact and took a place of honor beside Perkin's new king (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:263)
identity_line: Larys Strong — According to Mushroom he originated the slander that Rhaenyra murdered Helaena.

fab_section_md:
- Dowager Queen of House Hightower and Helaena's mother, who cursed her rival on learning of her daughter's death (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:41)
- Gyldayn reasons that had Rhaenyra meant murder, Alicent would have been the one flung down (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:39)
identity_line: Alicent Hightower — Dowager Queen of House Hightower and Helaena's mother, who cursed her rival on learning of her daughter's death.

fab_section_md:
- Ser, aged twenty and a Queensguard for less than a moon, named Lord Commander after distinguishing himself in Flea Bottom and recovering Ser Lorent's body (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:97)
- One of the Seven Who Rode, he was set afire, thrown, and torn to pieces by the mob (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:177)
- He had been Lord Commander for less than a day (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:177)
identity_line: Glendon Goode — Ser, aged twenty and a Queensguard for less than a moon, named Lord Commander after distinguishing himself in Flea Bottom and recovering Ser Lorent's body.

fab_section_md:
- Lord and the queen's master of coin, who proposed a tax on bastards (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:17)
- His manse was overrun; he was bound to a post and tortured until he revealed his hidden wealth (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:49)
identity_line: Bartimos Celtigar — Lord and the queen's master of coin, who proposed a tax on bastards.

fab_section_md:
- A boy of fifteen who made a brave stand in a doorway of the Celtigar manse (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:49)
- A treacherous serving girl let the rioters in, and he was slain by a spear through the back (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:49)
identity_line: Arthor Celtigar — A boy of fifteen who made a brave stand in a doorway of the Celtigar manse.

fab_section_md: - Ser, Lord Commander of the Queensguard, who led a hundred knights and men-at-arms into Flea Bottom (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:91)
identity_line: Lorent Marbrand — Ser, Lord Commander of the Queensguard, who led a hundred knights and men-at-arms into Flea Bottom.

fab_section_md:
- He was one of the Seven Who Rode and named the heir to White Harbor (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:147)
- He fled the city with the queen (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:245)

- Ser, who led his northmen down the Hook and found Fishermonger's Square swarming with Perkin's gutter knights (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:91)
- He lost a quarter of his men fighting back to the Red Keep (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:91)
identity_line: Torrhen Manderly — Ser, who led his northmen down the Hook and found Fishermonger's Square swarming with Perkin's gutter knights.

fab_section_md: - He was dragged before Perkin and beheaded (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:251)

- Ser, captain of the gold cloaks at the Iron Gate, who with Ser Garth restored some order north of Rhaenys's Hill (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:89)
- Rhaenyra gave him command of the gold cloaks (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:97)
identity_line: Balon Byrch — Ser, captain of the gold cloaks at the Iron Gate, who with Ser Garth restored some order north of Rhaenys's Hill.

fab_section_md:
- A woodsman who leapt onto Shrykos's neck and drove his axe into the beast's skull, striking seven blows for the Seven (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:195)
- Munkun mistakenly credits him also with Syrax (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:239)
identity_line: Hobb the Hewer — A woodsman who leapt onto Shrykos's neck and drove his axe into the beast's skull, striking seven blows for the Seven.

fab_section_md:
- Helaena's dragon, atop the Hill of Rhaenys, who snapped two chains at the queen's death (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:39)
- The last of the four pit dragons to die, she slew more than the other three combined before bringing down the Dragonpit's dome upon herself (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:211)
- A crossbow bolt in her eye drove her into the dome, which collapsed and crushed her (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:219)
identity_line: Dreamfyre — Helaena's dragon, atop the Hill of Rhaenys, who snapped two chains at the queen's death.

fab_section_md:
- Queen Rhaenyra's yellow she-dragon, who had never known another rider (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:157)
- She twisted beneath Joffrey and cast him off to his death (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:161)
- Riderless, she descended on the Shepherd's mobs at the Dragonpit and was slain by conflicting accounts (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:239)
identity_line: Syrax — Queen Rhaenyra's yellow she-dragon, who had never known another rider.

fab_section_md: - Prince Joffrey's dragon, housed in the Dragonpit, who retreated into his lair roasting his attackers (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:203)
identity_line: Tyraxes — Prince Joffrey's dragon, housed in the Dragonpit, who retreated into his lair roasting his attackers.

fab_section_md: - A pit dragon, the first to succumb, slain by Hobb the Hewer's axe if Eustace is believed (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:195)
identity_line: Shrykos — A pit dragon, the first to succumb, slain by Hobb the Hewer's axe if Eustace is believed.

fab_section_md: - A pit dragon slain by the Burning Knight, who thrust a spear into the beast's eye even as its flame devoured him (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:199)
identity_line: Morghul — A pit dragon slain by the Burning Knight, who thrust a spear into the beast's eye even as its flame devoured him.

fab_section_md: - Ser, one of the Sea Snake's sworn swords who cut into the dungeons to free Corlys and was hanged at dawn (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:21)
identity_line: Denys Woodwright — Ser, one of the Sea Snake's sworn swords who cut into the dungeons to free Corlys and was hanged at dawn.

fab_section_md: - Ser, sworn sword of the Sea Snake, whose plot was betrayed by a whore he had been bedding (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:21)
identity_line: Thoron True — Ser, sworn sword of the Sea Snake, whose plot was betrayed by a whore he had been bedding.

fab_section_md: - A crossbowman who boasted of killing Syrax until a loyalist cut out his tongue (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:239)
identity_line: Bean — A crossbowman who boasted of killing Syrax until a loyalist cut out his tongue.

fab_section_md: - A Dornish whore, reputed author of Gaemon's edicts and paramour of the king's mother Essie, if Mushroom is believed (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:275)
identity_line: Sylvenna Sand — A Dornish whore, reputed author of Gaemon's edicts and paramour of the king's mother Essie, if Mushroom is believed.

fab_section_md: - Suspected turncloak who fled before he could be put to the question; his flight taken as proof of guilt (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:17)
identity_line: Addam Velaryon — Suspected turncloak who fled before he could be put to the question; his flight taken as proof of guilt.

fab_section_md: - A huge armored brute who rushed into Morghul's flame and thrust his spear into the dragon's eye (fab-rhaenyra-overthrown-18-p01:199)
identity_line: the Burning Knight — A huge armored brute who rushed into Morghul's flame and thrust his spear into the dragon's eye.
